name: Feature development

approved-discovery: the problem is stated and falsifiable
the target user or segment is named
the desired outcome and a success signal are defined
the opportunity is sized well enough to justify continuing
approved-product-definition: the MVP scope is bounded — in-scope and out-of-scope are explicit
requirements are prioritised (e.g. MoSCoW) with a stated rationale
each requirement traces to a discovery need
measurable success criteria or target metrics are defined
approved-spec: every requirement has testable acceptance criteria
scope and target user are unambiguous
the spec is consistent with the approved product definition (no contradiction)
open questions are resolved or logged as assumptions with an owner
architecture-validated: the design meets the spec's non-functional requirements (scalability, availability, latency) with stated evidence or rationale
it is maintainable and modular — clear boundaries, no needless coupling
any architecture new to the project is justified by an ADR (else the unfamiliar-architecture stop condition fires)
security-relevant design choices (trust boundaries, authz, data handling) are identified for the security gate
release-candidate-ready: the verify report's overall-result is pass with accepted sign-off
release notes or a changelog entry are drafted
no open high or critical security finding remains
deploy-target-configured: at least one deploy target (k8s/aws/gcp/azure) is declared in .praxis/config.json under deploy.targets (else the optional step is skipped and that skip is recorded — never assume a cloud target)
the target's environment, Terraform directory, and deployment strategy are resolved, with state isolated per target and per environment
an MCP execution backend is configured and reachable, or the phase falls back to plan-only (no apply)
explicit promotion criteria and a rollback trigger are defined for the target, and any production target requires a manual human promotion decision
deploy-healthy: terraform apply and the workload rollout completed atomically (fully succeeded or rolled back)
post-apply health meets the promotion criteria (readiness green, error rate and latency within budget for the stated window)
the deploy report records target, image/SHA, plan summary, promotion decision, health evidence, and the verbatim rollback command

predicate: every acceptance criterion in the spec is demonstrably met
the full test suite passes with no regressions
no unaddressed high or critical security finding remains (each is fixed or carries an approved risk-acceptance decision)
performance-sensitive surfaces meet their stated budget with no regression beyond threshold

G-tests.description: Test suite passes with no regressions.

G-assets-present.passCriteria: every asset named in the experience contract resolves

G-imports-used.description: Named primitives/modules are implemented or imported where expected.
G-imports-used.passCriteria: no named dependency is missing or substituted

G-acceptance.description: Every acceptance criterion in the spec is demonstrably met.

G-security.description: Security review found no unaddressed high or critical vulnerability (OWASP Top 10, authn/authz, secrets, dependency/supply-chain).
G-security.passCriteria: a security review is recorded and every high/critical finding is fixed or carries an approved risk-acceptance decision

G-performance.description: Performance review found no regression beyond the change's stated budget (latency, throughput, resource use).
G-performance.passCriteria: performance-sensitive surfaces meet their stated budget with evidence; none regress beyond threshold

G-visual.description: Visual output matches the approved reference.

stopConditions: the problem, target user, or desired outcome is unclear after discovery
product definition is attempted before discovery is approved
specification is attempted before research findings are recorded (research must precede spec)
a spec requirement does not trace to an approved product-definition need
spec scope or target user is ambiguous
task decomposition is attempted before architecture is validated
the plan would commit to an architecture the project has not used
a gate's required decision is still pending (pending is not approved)
a gate fails and work continues without returning to its mapped state for rework (a failed gate must route back via transitions.onGateFailure, never be bypassed)
release is attempted with an unaddressed high or critical security finding and no approved risk-acceptance decision
release is attempted before the verify report shows acceptance criteria are met
deploy is attempted to a cloud target not declared in .praxis/config.json (never assume a target — the optional deploy step is skipped and recorded when no target is configured)
deploy applies to a production target without an explicit human promotion decision or a defined rollback trigger
